our_argument_summary: We claim aberration is achromatic while refraction is chromatic (citing Bradley 1728), invoke Kramers-Kronig relations to close the 'achromatic aether' escape, and cite VLBI + Gaia as confirming orbital-velocity origin. Verdict: Refuted by Data.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-016 is listed as a static one-line entry in the dome's WIN list: 'Annual Aberration Refractive Model CONFIRMED (alpha = 2.56e-8, 20.5 arcsec)'. No derivation, formula, or validation logic is present in any available dome source file. The alpha value is asserted without connecting it to dome geometric parameters.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely hardcoded/declarative. The alpha = 2.56e-8 value appears as a static assertion. No monitoring domain, no API call, no computational validation exists for this WIN in the dome's automation pipeline.
data_sources_used: None — no external data is fetched or compared
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the claim is never computationally tested
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None identified. Our code_analysis tags correctly reflect the hardcoded, unmonitored status.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-016 appears only as a static one-liner in the dome's confirmed_wins listing. No monitor.py domain, no API call, no fetch logic. The value 'alpha = 2.56e-8, 20.5 arcsec' is a static string. Classification: hardcoded (pred=obs with both baked in, no external validation).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Stellar aberration has been explained by Earth's orbital velocity since Bradley (1728). The aberration constant κ ≈ 20.4955 arcsec = v/c where v ≈ 29.78 km/s is a textbook result. The dome renames the cause from 'orbital velocity' to 'aetheric refractive perturbation' but reproduces the same number.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Stellar aberration was discovered in 1728 and its constant precisely measured ever since (current IAU value: 20.49552 arcsec at J2000). The dome model adopted this known value as a 'prediction' — the evolution log shows this WIN appeared in v48.0. The observation predates the dome claim by ~298 years.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation connects dome geometric parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, etc.) to alpha = 2.56e-8 or to the 20.5 arcsec aberration constant. The value appears to be reverse-engineered from the known aberration constant. No formula path dome_geometry → alpha → aberration_angle exists in any dome source file.

new_issues_introduced: Kramers-Kronig sentence duplicated verbatim — copy-paste error during patching

holes_found.description: DUPLICATE TEXT: The Kramers-Kronig sentence appears twice verbatim in detail_evidence: 'Moreover, the Kramers-Kronig relations require that any causal physical medium with a real refractive index n > 1 must also be dispersive (wavelength-dependent): a perfectly achromatic aether with n > 1 violates causality and cannot exist as a physical medium.' This is a copy-paste error introduced when applying Cycle 1 patches. It makes the review look sloppy — a dome defender could point to this as evidence of careless scholarship, undermining our credibility on the very WIN where we invoke sophisticated physics (Kramers-Kronig).
holes_found.recommendation: Remove the second occurrence of the Kramers-Kronig sentence. The text should read: '...while stellar aberration is achromatic. Moreover, the Kramers-Kronig relations require... cannot exist as a physical medium. Modern VLBI measurements...'

holes_found.description: Missing radio-optical frequency agreement argument (carried over from Cycle 1). VLBI operates at GHz frequencies (~cm wavelengths) while optical observations are at ~500 THz (~500 nm wavelengths) — a factor of ~10^6 in frequency. Both yield the same aberration constant to milliarcsecond precision. Even weakly dispersive materials show measurable index variation across a factor of 2 in frequency. Agreement across six orders of magnitude is devastating to any refractive mechanism and would significantly strengthen the Kramers-Kronig argument that is now in our text.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence after the Kramers-Kronig argument: 'Empirically, VLBI (GHz) and optical (~500 THz) observations — spanning six orders of magnitude in frequency — yield the same aberration constant to milliarcsecond precision, ruling out any refractive mechanism regardless of the medium.'

holes_found.description: Missing circularity argument (carried over from Cycle 1). The dome's alpha = 2.56e-8 is not independently derived from dome geometric parameters. No published formula connects disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome parameter to this alpha value. The most likely origin is reverse-engineering from the known aberration constant κ ≈ 20.4955 arcsec ≈ v/c. This makes the 'prediction' circular: the dome takes the known answer and relabels it as a dome parameter. The dome's evolution log confirms this WIN appeared in v48.0, long after the aberration constant was precisely known.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence or detail_extra: 'No derivation connects dome geometry to alpha = 2.56e-8; the value appears reverse-engineered from the known aberration constant κ = v/c, making the claim circular.'

holes_found.description: The VLBI link (https://ivscc.gsfc.nasa.gov/about/vlbi/) is informational but doesn't cite a specific measurement confirming aberration. A more targeted reference would be Titov et al. (2011, A&A 529, A91) which uses VLBI to measure secular aberration drift, directly confirming the orbital-velocity mechanism.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding a more specific VLBI citation for aberration measurement.

stronger_arguments.description: Annual variation without annual cause. The dome model claims the aetheric medium has a static refractive index alpha = 2.56e-8, yet stellar aberration varies sinusoidally with a period of exactly one year. A static refractive medium cannot produce a time-varying apparent displacement. The dome must either invoke a time-varying aether (which requires a mechanism with an annual period — suspicious for a model denying Earth's orbit) or explain how a static medium creates a periodic effect. This is a logical gap the dome cannot close without implicitly reintroducing orbital motion.
sources: Standard aberration theory — the annual period is the signature of orbital motion

stronger_arguments.description: Radio-optical wavelength agreement spanning 10^6 in frequency. VLBI (centimeter wavelengths) and optical/Gaia (nanometer wavelengths) yield the same aberration constant. The Kramers-Kronig relations guarantee any physical medium with n > 1 must be dispersive, but the empirical agreement across six orders of magnitude in frequency is the observational nail in the coffin.
sources: Titov et al. 2011, A&A 529, A91 (VLBI secular aberration drift)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies stellar aberration as a real phenomenon with a well-established constant of ~20.5 arcseconds. The underlying physics — that apparent stellar positions shift annually — is genuine. The numerical value they cite (20.5 arcsec) is accurate to the precision stated.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Citing an accurate observational value doesn't constitute a prediction. The aberration constant has been known since 1728. The dome provides no derivation connecting alpha = 2.56e-8 to dome geometry, no mechanism for the annual periodicity without orbital motion, and no way to reconcile an achromatic effect with any causal refractive medium (Kramers-Kronig). The 'prediction' is the known answer with a new label.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 review of WIN-016 finds the verdict (Refuted by Data) continues to hold firmly. Three of the four Cycle 1 recommendations were successfully applied: the sentence fragment was fixed, Bradley's date corrected to 1728, and the Kramers-Kronig argument added. However, a copy-paste error during patching introduced a DUPLICATE of the Kramers-Kronig sentence — the exact same two-sentence passage appears twice consecutively in detail_evidence. This is the most urgent fix (severity: major, not for scientific reasons but for credibility — a professional review with verbatim duplicate paragraphs invites dismissal).

Two Cycle 1 recommendations remain unimplemented: (1) the radio-optical frequency agreement argument (VLBI at GHz vs optical at ~500 THz, same aberration constant across 10^6 in frequency), and (2) noting the circularity of the alpha value (no derivation from dome geometry, appears reverse-engineered from the known constant). Both would meaningfully strengthen an already-sound argument.

A new stronger argument identified in Cycle 2: the annual periodicity problem. The dome claims a static refractive index alpha = 2.56e-8, but stellar aberration varies sinusoidally with a one-year period. A static medium cannot produce a time-varying displacement. The dome must either invoke a time-varying aether (requiring an annual mechanism — which looks suspiciously like orbital motion in disguise) or explain how a constant refractive index creates a periodic apparent shift. This logical gap is independent of the Kramers-Kronig argument and attacks the claim from a different angle.

Code analysis tags remain accurate: monitoring=hardcoded (static assertion, no computational validation), relabels_standard=true (textbook aberration constant renamed), post_hoc=true (observation known since 1728, dome claim from v48.0), derives_from_dome=false (no derivation connecting dome geometry to alpha).

citations_verified: VLBI link (https://ivscc.gsfc.nasa.gov/about/vlbi/) — resolves correctly to IVS/GSFC VLBI information page
Gaia link (https://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia) — resolves to ESA Gaia mission page
Bradley (1728) — correct date for the presentation of his aberration explanation (Phil. Trans. 1729, observations 1725-1728)
citations_unchecked: Kramers-Kronig relations claim — the physics is standard textbook material (Jackson Ch. 7.10, Toll 1956) but no specific citation is given in our text. Consider adding one for readers unfamiliar with the relations.
